originalAlarm.description: Potential leak of memory pointed to by 'buf'

reasoning: buf is allocated via calloc (through apr_rmm_addr_get) at L16 inside #if APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY, used in printf at L17, but never freed — genuine memory leak. No ERROR comment in file and no existing TRUE record for d05e574ec26.c, so this is a new bug not in the VBDB. The PC (APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY=T) exactly captures the condition — allocation and leak only occur under #if APR_HAS_SHARED_MEMORY. Correctly constrained.
